title: phaleria macrocarpa
intro: Phaleria macrocarpa, also known as the 'Frangipani of the East Indies', is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Phaleria macrocarpa include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
